patterns.label: Meeting Notes & Action Item Capture
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 45 confirmed +6 (Recurring Loop Meeting Notes Maintenance doc freq=4 +4, Discussion-to-Meeting-to-Doc partial +1, DevOps-Alert-to-Sync partial +1). 353 total, 65.71 hrs. STABLE — Loop doc meeting notes workflow (Agenda → Notes → Decisions → Follow-up tasks) remains strongest chain head in catalog. 28-cycle gap since cycle 17 update not reflected in signal data — pattern continuously active per WorkIQ signals.

patterns.label: Azure DevOps Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 45 confirmed +30 (Azure DevOps Eval Notification Triage email freq=30 +30). 925 total, 32.72 hrs. STABLE — freq=30 matches baseline. Subject clusters: offline evals, LIFT, FTD, CMD, evaluation pipeline. DEEP SATURATION past 920. Decomposition into 5 sub-classifiers overdue 28+ cycles. Highest automationScore in catalog (95).

RISING — EvalCon 2026-04-02 deadline (13 days) amplifying. Project Crucible, FY26 agent evaluation, customer donated evals governance all active. Highest valueScore in catalog (95).

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Externalizes PM's eval SME knowledge to answer inbound questions about eval design, test type selection, threshold guidance, multi-turn/file-based/tool-trigger evals, and results interpretation. Breaks a BSI 92 CRITICAL bottleneck.
triggerExamples: How do I design evals for an agent that uses multiple tools?
What threshold should I set for my eval to be meaningful?
My eval results show 40% failure — what does that actually mean and what should I do?
What's the difference between file-based and tool-trigger evals?
Can you help me generate an eval set for our customer pilot?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Breaks BSI 92 CRITICAL bottleneck at 15 inbound/week, PM as sole DRI. Unblocks entire 7-pattern Eval Ecosystem. Each deflected T1 request saves 9 minutes. At 15/week: 2+ hours recovered immediately. Highest ROI skill in catalog.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Captures meeting output into structured Loop document: Agenda, Notes, Decisions, Open Questions, Follow-up tasks with owners and due dates.
triggerExamples: Meeting just ended — capture the notes and action items.
Generate a Loop doc from this meeting summary.
Extract decisions and follow-ups from this transcript.
Populate the 1:1 Loop doc with today's discussion points.
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Chain head for 6-pattern meeting output ecosystem. 353 occurrences, 65.71 hrs, 28 cycles confirmed. SGI CRITICAL (92) — meeting notes structure is the top standardization gap. Production-ready.

candidateSkills.name: eval-report-synthesizer
candidateSkills.description: Synthesizes eval results into structured analysis: pass/fail breakdown, root-cause, remediation playbook, coverage model assessment, executive one-pager.
triggerExamples: Eval results are in — generate the analysis report.
What do these eval scores actually mean and what should we do?
Create an exec-ready summary of this eval run for the EvalCon presentation.
Synthesize the eval strategy thread into a briefing doc.
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Highest valueScore in catalog (95). RISING with EvalCon 2026-04-02 deadline (13 days). 450 occurrences, 116.77 hrs. Deadline amplification active.

candidateSkills.name: voc-exec-summary-generator
candidateSkills.description: Synthesizes customer VoC signals into exec-ready one-liners, MPR bullets, and leadership blurbs. Deduplicates overlapping customer asks.
triggerExamples: Give me one line on eval status for the MPR.
Write the MPR blurb for our eval engagement with this customer.
Are these two customer asks the same thing or different?
I need 3 bullets on customer eval pain points for the leadership review deck.
candidateSkills.valueProposition: 4 confirmed instances/week. Templatable structured output. Downstream to MPR cycles and leadership reviews. Quick-win skill with immediate measurable impact.

candidateSkills.name: weekly-status-report-generator
candidateSkills.description: Takes a single done/next/blockers input and generates consistent weekly status updates formatted for Loop document, Teams chat narrative, and executive email digest.
triggerExamples: Generate my weekly status update from these notes.
I've filled in done/next/blockers — format it for the Loop doc and the Teams channel.
Take this rough status and produce the three different formats we need.
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates multi-format duplication (same content authored 3 times). SGI CRITICAL. 154 occurrences. Team-wide standardization opportunity — multiple people doing this independently.

spawnRegistry.description: Parent-child pattern lineage. Tracks when a parent pattern sheds a sub-signal into a standalone pattern.
